design_philosophy.core_loop: Execute football plays while managing field hazards, opponent violence, and dirty tricks to achieve touchdowns and eliminate enemy players
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing traditional football strategy against chaotic environmental hazards and opponent-controlled sabotage mechanics that can instantly kill players and cause fumbles
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing violent post-apocalyptic football where winning through lethal force is mechanically equivalent to traditional scoring
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rejects sanitized sports simulation in favor of brutal, consequence-filled gameplay where player death is a core mechanic and the referee is corruptible. Takes John Madden Football's engine and inverts its philosophy: strategy remains high but is constantly threatened by environmental chaos and 'Nasty Audibles' that weaponize the sport itself

difficulty.lives_system: Players can die mid-play causing fumbles; killing enough opposing players forces forfeit
difficulty.checkpointing: Pickup game or full-season structure; individual plays are discrete units
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players can use Nasty Audibles (dirty tricks like exploding balls, invisibility, electric shocks, jet packs) twice per half; can bribe referee twice per game for phony penalties; field hazards (landmines, fire pits, toxic waste) are environmental constraints rather than difficulty settings

progression.unlock_mechanism: Season mode progresses through games toward championship; winning championship results in MVP induction into 'Hole of Flame' (death ceremony)
replayability_hooks: Multiple teams with distinct rosters (aliens, skeletons, robots, trolls, superhumans)
Different field types (rock, ice, toxic waste, rubber) alter hazard profiles
Pickup game mode allows immediate play without progression
Humorous player names and team compositions encourage team experimentation
Strategic depth in play-calling versus opponent Nasty Audibles

world_building_techniques: Post-apocalyptic setting established through radiation-mutated player types (aliens, skeletons, robots, trolls)
Field hazards (landmines, fire pits, toxic waste surfaces, empty space gaps) communicate hostile world through environmental design
Instruction manual provides lore about alien invasion, chaos, and lost history explaining the mutant state
Team names (Deathskin Razors, Midway Monsters) and player name parodies (Bones Jackson, L.T. Impaler) build tone through dark humor
Championship victory results in MVP's induction into 'Hole of Flame' — ritual death ceremony that reinforces stakes

faction_indicators: Seven player teams composed of mutant archetypes (aliens, skeletons, robots, trolls, superhumans)
Team affiliation determines roster composition and player abilities
Ref-bribing mechanics suggest organizational corruption and factional alliances
danger_signals: Field hazards (landmines, fire pits, toxic waste, open chasms) indicate environmental hostility
Player death mechanic signals lethal stakes
Nasty Audibles weaponize the sport itself (exploding balls, invisibility, electric shocks, jet packs as gameplay tools)
Referee bribery and subsequent ref-killing creates lawless power dynamics
environmental_storytelling: Varied field types (rock, ice, toxic waste, rubber) suggest different ecological zones within post-apocalyptic world
Hazard placement forces adaptation of traditional football strategy
MVP induction ceremony (stuffing winner into firepit) depicts civilization as brutally hierarchical
